locator: Art. 1(1), Basic Law for the Federal Republic of Germany, 23 May 1949; official English translation, Deutscher Bundestag, 2012 ed.
text: Human dignity shall be inviolable. To respect and protect it shall be the duty of all state authority.

locator: Art. 79(3), Basic Law for the Federal Republic of Germany, 23 May 1949; official English translation, Deutscher Bundestag, 2012 ed.
text: Amendments to this Basic Law affecting the division of the Federation into Länder, their participation on principle in the legislative process, or the principles laid down in Articles 1 and 20 shall be inadmissible.

locator: Regierungserklärung des Bundeskanzlers, Konrad Adenauer, 20 September 1949; in Verhandlungen des Deutschen Bundestages, 1. Wahlperiode, 5. Sitzung, p. 22; English translation in Adenauer, Konrad, Memoirs 1945–53 (London: Weidenfeld & Nicolson, 1966)
text: The Federal Government is determined to maintain democracy and to defend it against all attacks. It will act in accordance with the principles of the rule of law, which is the foundation of a free state.
